message:
🐛 Remove read:user OAuth scope — only request user:email (#2521)

The console only uses public profile fields (id, login, avatar_url) from
GitHub's GET /user endpoint, which are available without any user scope.
The read:user scope grants access to private profile data that the console
never reads, violating the principle of least privilege.

Changes:
- Remove read:user from OAuth scopes, keeping only user:email
- Add GET /user/emails fallback when GET /user returns no public email,
  using the primary verified email (requires user:email scope)
- Update error message referencing the old scope

Closes #2369

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
